label: About
title: About Crossfade Firework Corridor
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 14 recent saved-signal files and 8 captured links, then narrowed the page to 6 source windows around @loink_loe2: my uncle watching 9/11 at my little cousins basketball game lmao and Drafted - Free AI House Plans & Downloads, plus 4 more. A source image from business.x.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Crossfade Firework Corridor: A single finished still image of a public interior corridor at night, built around two enormous clean white diagonal threshold beams crossing over a scratched black field. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 6 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against left tilted torn photographic slab, central etched black floor-plan slab, and upper-center luminous motion arrows.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
